revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町道土井郷324-18 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（10月29、30日） }}
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]道土井（みちどい）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大山祇命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主命]]を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神の事代主神は、[[合祀]]された蛭子神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。往古、当郷は塩釜（土居）が3つあったことから「'''三ツ土居'''（みつどい）」と称されており、これが訛って現在の呼び方となり、近年「道土井」の字を当てたものである。当社はこの「三ツ土居」と薪炭林の守護神として奉祀されたと伝えられる。また、塩釜1ヵ所ごとに「塩釜（土居）神」の祠が祀られている。 [[ファイル:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に[[無格社]]に列せられ、［［大正］］2年（［［1913年］］）10月30日に郷内・蛭子神社を合祀した。現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。
== 祭祀 == 10月29、30日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[例大祭]]（10月29、30日）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する今里郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)| 大山祇神社]]'''、浜ノ浦郷に'''[[天満神社 (新上五島町)|天満神社]]'''が、旧[[若松町]]荒川郷に''' [[雄嶽日枝神社]] '''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社 画像: IMG_arakawa.JPG|雄嶽日枝神社</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町道土井郷324-18 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（10月29、30日） }}

== 祭祀 == 10月29、30日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。

== その他の神社 == 隣接する今里郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)| 大山祇神社]]'''、続浜ノ浦郷に'''[[天満神社 (新上五島町)|天満神社]]'''が、旧[[若松町]]荒川郷に''' [[雄嶽日枝神社]] '''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） 画像: IMG_arakawa.JPG|雄嶽日枝神社（荒川郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町道土井郷324-18 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（10月29、30日） }}
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]道土井郷（みちどいごう）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主|事代主命]]を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神の事代主神は、[[合祀]]された蛭子神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。往古、当郷は塩釜（土居）が3つあったことから「'''三ツ土居'''（みつどい）」と称されており、これが訛って現在の呼び方となり、近年「道土井」の字を当てたものである。当社はこの「三ツ土居」と薪炭林の守護神として奉祀されたと伝えられる。また、塩釜1ヵ所ごとに「塩釜（土居）神」の祠が祀られている。 [[ファイル:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に[[無格社]]に列せられ、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）10月30日に郷内・蛭子神社を合祀した。現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。
== 祭祀 == 10月29、30日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[例大祭]]（10月29、30日）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する今里郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)| 大山祇神社]]'''、続浜ノ浦郷に'''[[天満神社 (新上五島町)|天満神社]]'''が、旧[[若松町]]荒川郷に'''[[雄嶽日枝神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） 画像: IMG_hamano.JPG|天満神社（続浜ノ浦郷） 画像: IMG_arakawa.JPG|雄嶽日枝神社（荒川郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 祭神 == [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主|事代主命]]を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神の事代主神は、[[合祀]]された蛭子神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。往古、当郷は塩釜（土居）が3つあったことから「'''三ツ土居'''（みつどい）」と称されており、これが訛って現在の呼び方となり、近年「道土井」の字を当てたものである。当社はこの「三ツ土居」と薪炭林の守護神として奉祀されたと伝えられる。また、塩釜1ヵ所ごとに「塩釜（土居）神」の祠が祀られている。 [[ファイル:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に[[無格社]]に列せられ、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）10月30日に郷内・蛭子神社を合祀した。現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。
== 祭祀 == 10月29、30日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
; 主な祭礼・神事 *[[例大祭]]（10月29、30日）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)| 大山祇神社]]'''、[[続浜ノ浦郷]]に'''[[天満神社 (新上五島町)|天満神社]]'''が、[[荒川郷]]に'''[[雄嶽日枝神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） 画像: IMG_hamano.JPG|天満神社（続浜ノ浦郷） 画像: IMG_arakawa.JPG|雄嶽日枝神社（荒川郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長崎県}}

{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町道土井郷324-18 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（10月29、30日） }}
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[道土井郷]]（みちどいごう）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主|事代主命]]を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神の事代主神は、[[合祀]]された蛭子神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。往古、当郷は塩釜（土居）が3つあったことから「'''三ツ土居'''（みつどい）」と称されており、これが訛って現在の呼び方となり、近年「道土井」の字を当てたものである。当社はこの「三ツ土居」と薪炭林の守護神として奉祀されたと伝えられる。また、塩釜1ヵ所ごとに「塩釜（土居）神」の祠が祀られている。 [[ファイル:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に[[無格社]]に列せられ、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）10月30日に郷内・蛭子神社を合祀した。現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。
== 祭祀 == 10月29、30日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
; 主な祭礼・神事 *[[例大祭]]（10月29、30日）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)| 大山祇神社]]'''、[[続浜ノ浦郷]]に'''[[天満神社 (新上五島町)|天満神社]]'''が、[[荒川郷]]に'''[[雄嶽日枝神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） 画像: IMG_hamano.JPG|天満神社（続浜ノ浦郷） 画像: IMG_arakawa.JPG|雄嶽日枝神社（荒川郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長崎県}}

revision.text: == Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. *[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== 祭神 == The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== 祭祀 == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

*[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== 祭神 == The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== 祭祀 == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== 祭神 == The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== 祭祀 == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== 祭祀 == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30

*[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== 祭祀 == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

[[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== 祭祀 == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

[[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=draft}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|12=draft}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=draft}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagasaki}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
8 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷) up to rev 98956133
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町道土井郷324-18 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（10月29、30日） }}
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[道土井郷]]（みちどいごう）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主|事代主命]]を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神の事代主神は、[[合祀]]された蛭子神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。往古、当郷は塩釜（土居）が3つあったことから「'''三ツ土居'''（みつどい）」と称されており、これが訛って現在の呼び方となり、近年「道土井」の字を当てたものである。当社はこの「三ツ土居」と薪炭林の守護神として奉祀されたと伝えられる。また、塩釜1ヵ所ごとに「塩釜（土居）神」の祠が祀られている。 [[ファイル:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]] [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に[[無格社]]に列せられ、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）10月30日に郷内・蛭子神社を合祀した。現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。
== 祭祀 == 10月29、30日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
; 主な祭礼・神事 *[[例大祭]]（10月29、30日）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)| 大山祇神社]]'''、[[続浜ノ浦郷]]に'''[[天満神社 (新上五島町)|天満神社]]'''が、[[荒川郷]]に'''[[雄嶽日枝神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） 画像: IMG_hamano.JPG|天満神社（続浜ノ浦郷） 画像: IMG_arakawa.JPG|雄嶽日枝神社（荒川郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長崎県}}

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine Ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30

*
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine Ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**

== 参考文献 == * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

revision.text:
*
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine Ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|qq=}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|qq=}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otake Hie Shrine|qq=}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

*
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_michidoi1.JPG|250px]] |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{coord|32|56|40.6|N|129|02|01.2|E|scale:25000}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine Ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|qq=}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|qq=}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otake Hie Shrine|qq=}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG michidoi1.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.944611|N|129.033667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in **Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** as its main deity, alongside **Kotoshironushi** as an auxiliary deity.
The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine Ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|qq=}}** – Located in **Imazato-gō** * **{{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|qq=}}** – Located in **Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otake Hie Shrine|qq=}}** – Located in **Arakawa-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

The **auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi** was originally the enshrined deity of **Ebisu Shrine**, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. Historically, this region was known as **"Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居)**, meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into **"Michidoi" (道土井)**. The shrine is believed to have been established as a **guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests**. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the **Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami)** exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)** under the **modern shrine Ranking system**. On **October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2)**, it merged with **Ebisu Shrine**, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the **annual festival** held on **October 29–30**, **Kamigotō Kagura** is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a **Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset**, included as part of **Gotō Kagura**. Additionally, it has been **individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture**.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **Annual Grand Festival (例大祭)** – October 29–30

Kamigotō Kagura is a Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset, included as part of Gotō Kagura. Additionally, it has been individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture.

{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG michidoi1.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.944611|N|129.033667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines Ōyamatsumi as its main deity, alongside Kotoshironushi as an auxiliary deity.
The auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi was originally the enshrined deity of Ebisu Shrine, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. Historically, this region was known as "Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居), meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into "Michidoi" (道土井). The shrine is believed to have been established as a guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami) exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was classified as an unranked shrine (mukakusha) under the modern shrine Ranking system. On October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2), it merged with Ebisu Shrine, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In 1952 (Shōwa 27), the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the annual festival held on October 29–30, Kamigotō Kagura is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset, included as part of Gotō Kagura. Additionally, it has been individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * Annual Grand Festival (例大祭) – October 29–30
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|qq=|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}} – Located in Imazato-gō * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|qq=|1=Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442852}} – Located in Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō * {{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otake Hie Shrine|qq=|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} – Located in Arakawa-gō

== Bibliography == * "Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16) – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * "Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3) – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * "Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition) – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG michidoi1.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.944611|N|129.033667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines Ōyamatsumi as its main deity, alongside Kotoshironushi as an auxiliary deity.
The auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi was originally the enshrined deity of Ebisu Shrine, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. Historically, this region was known as "Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居), meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into "Michidoi" (道土井). The shrine is believed to have been established as a guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami) exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was classified as an unranked shrine (mukakusha) under the modern shrine Ranking system. On October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2), it merged with Ebisu Shrine, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In 1952 (Shōwa 27), the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the annual festival held on October 29–30, Kamigotō Kagura is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset, included as part of Gotō Kagura. Additionally, it has been individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * Annual Grand Festival (例大祭) – October 29–30
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|qq=|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}} – Located in Imazato-gō * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|qq=|1=Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442852}} – Located in Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō * {{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otake Hie Shrine|qq=|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} – Located in Arakawa-gō

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines Ōyamatsumi as its main deity, alongside Kotoshironushi as an auxiliary deity.
The auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi was originally the enshrined deity of Ebisu Shrine, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. Historically, this region was known as "Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居), meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into "Michidoi" (道土井). The shrine is believed to have been established as a guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami) exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was classified as an unranked shrine (mukakusha) under the modern shrine Ranking system. On October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2), it merged with Ebisu Shrine, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In 1952 (Shōwa 27), the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the annual festival held on October 29–30, Kamigotō Kagura is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset, included as part of Gotō Kagura. Additionally, it has been individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * Annual Grand Festival (例大祭) – October 29–30
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}} – Located in Imazato-gō * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|1=Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442852}} – Located in Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō * {{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} – Located in Arakawa-gō

{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG michidoi1.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Michidoi-gō 324-18 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.944611|N|129.033667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= October 30 |ceremonies= Kamigotō Kagura (October 29–30) |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Michidoi-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines Ōyamatsumi as its main deity, alongside Kotoshironushi as an auxiliary deity.
The auxiliary deity Kotoshironushi was originally the enshrined deity of Ebisu Shrine, which was later merged with Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. Historically, this region was known as "Mitsudoi" (三ツ土居), meaning "Three Salt Kilns (Doi)," referring to the three salt-making sites in the area. Over time, the name evolved into "Michidoi" (道土井). The shrine is believed to have been established as a guardian shrine for Mitsudoi and for protecting the local charcoal and firewood forests. Additionally, small shrines dedicated to the Salt Kiln Deities (Shio-gama no Kami) exist at each of the former salt-making sites.
[[File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]] In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was classified as an unranked shrine (mukakusha) under the modern shrine Ranking system. On October 30, 1913 (Taishō 2), it merged with Ebisu Shrine, which was previously located in the same district. Following this merger, the shrine was relocated to its current site. In 1952 (Shōwa 27), the shrine building was reconstructed.
== Rites == During the annual festival held on October 29–30, Kamigotō Kagura is performed as an offering to the deities. Kamigotō Kagura is a Nationally Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset, included as part of Gotō Kagura. Additionally, it has been individually designated as an Intangible Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * Annual Grand Festival (例大祭) – October 29–30
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Imazato-gō)|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}} – Located in Imazato-gō * {{ill|Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|ja|天満神社 (新上五島町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigotō)|1=Tenman Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442852}} – Located in Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō * {{ill|Otake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} – Located in Arakawa-gō
<gallery> File:IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazato-gō) File:IMG_hamano.JPG|Tenman Shrine (Tsuzuku Hamano-ura-gō) File:IMG_arakawa.JPG|Otake Hie Shrine (Arakawa-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == * "Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16) – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * "Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3) – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * "Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition) – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
